question.text: Ankit earns 7% on his fixed deposit, but inflation is running at 6%. What is his approximate real rate of return?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Real return = Nominal return - Inflation rate. So 7% - 6% = 1%. The real rate of return reflects the actual increase in purchasing power after accounting for inflation. **The Trap:** Many candidates simply pick the nominal return (7%) or add both rates (13%). The real return formula subtracts inflation from nominal return. **Cross-Exam Context:** Inflation risk is a silent wealth destroyer. Even positive nominal returns can mean negative real returns if inflation is higher than the investment return. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Show clients this calculation to demonstrate why parking all money in FDs at 7% when inflation is 6% barely grows their real wealth. This motivates allocation to growth assets.
